matn_ar: َ ذَلِكَ كَانَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ رَجُلٍ مِنْ الْيَهُودِ أَرْضٌ فَجَحَدَنِي فَقَدَّمْتُهُ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ لِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَلَكَ بَيِّنَةٌ قُلْتُ لَا فَقَالَ لِلْيَهُودِيِّ احْلِفْ فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِذَنْ يَحْلِفَ فَيَذْهَبَ مَالِي فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ { إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ وَأَيْمَانِهِمْ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا } إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَةِ
matn_ar_clean: ذلك كان بيني وبين رجل من اليهود أرض فجحدني فقدمته إلى النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم فقال لي رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم ألك بينة قلت لا فقال لليهودي احلف فقلت يا رسول الله إذن يحلف فيذهب مالي فأنزل الله عز وجل { إن الذين يشترون بعهد الله وأيمانهم ثمنا قليلا } إلى آخر الآية
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Mu'awiya, from al-A'mash, from Shaqiq — that 'Abdullah said: 'the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: Whoever swears a false oath to seize the wealth of a Muslim will meet Allah (عز وجل) while He is angry with him.' Al-Ash'ath [ibn Qays] said: 'By Allah, this was about me! There was a dispute between me and a Jew over some land — he denied my claim. I brought him before the Prophet ﷺ. the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to me: Do you have a proof? I said: No. He said to the Jew: Take an oath. I said: O Messenger of Allah, if he takes an oath he will take my property. Then Allah (عز وجل) revealed: Indeed, those who sell the covenant of Allah and their oaths for a small price... [3:77] — to the end of the verse.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏عَاصِمٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زِرٍّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كُنْتُ ‏ ‏أَرْعَى غَنَمًا ‏ ‏لِعُقْبَةَ بْنِ أَبِي مُعَيْطٍ ‏ ‏فَمَرَّ بِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏وَأَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا غُلَامُ هَلْ مِنْ لَبَنٍ قَالَ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ وَلَكِنِّي مُؤْتَمَنٌ قَالَ فَهَلْ مِنْ شَاةٍ لَمْ ‏ ‏يَنْزُ ‏ ‏عَلَيْهَا ‏ ‏الْفَحْلُ ‏ ‏فَأَتَيْتُهُ بِشَاةٍ فَمَسَحَ ضَرْعَهَا فَنَزَلَ لَبَنٌ فَحَلَبَهُ فِي إِنَاءٍ فَشَرِبَ وَسَقَى ‏ ‏أَبَا بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏ثُمَّ قَالَ لِلضَّرْعِ ‏ ‏اقْلِصْ ‏ ‏فَقَلَصَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ثُمَّ أَتَيْتُهُ بَعْدَ هَذَا فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ عَلِّمْنِي مِنْ هَذَا الْقَوْلِ قَالَ فَمَسَحَ رَأْسِي وَقَالَ يَرْحَمُكَ اللَّهُ فَإِنَّكَ ‏ ‏غُلَيِّمٌ مُعَلَّمٌ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَفَّانُ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَاصِمٍ ‏ ‏بِإِسْنَادِهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏فَأَتَاهُ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏بِصَخْرَةٍ مَنْقُورَةٍ فَاحْتَلَبَ فِيهَا فَشَرِبَ وَشَرِبَ ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏وَشَرِبْتُ قَالَ ثُمَّ أَتَيْتُهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ قُلْتُ عَلِّمْنِي مِنْ هَذَا الْقُرْآنِ قَالَ إِنَّكَ غُلَامٌ مُعَلَّمٌ قَالَ فَأَخَذْتُ مِنْ ‏ ‏فِيهِ سَبْعِينَ سُورَةً ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ حَدَّثَنِي عَاصِمٌ عَنْ زِرٍّ عَنْ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ كُنْتُ أَرْعَى غَنَمًا لِعُقْبَةَ بْنِ أَبِي مُعَيْطٍ فَمَرَّ بِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَأَبُو بَكْرٍ فَقَالَ يَا غُلَامُ هَلْ مِنْ لَبَنٍ قَالَ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ وَلَكِنِّي مُؤْتَمَنٌ قَالَ فَهَلْ مِنْ شَاةٍ لَمْ يَنْزُ عَلَيْهَا الْفَحْلُ فَأَتَيْتُهُ بِشَاةٍ فَمَسَحَ ضَرْعَهَا فَنَزَلَ لَبَنٌ فَحَلَبَهُ فِي إِنَاءٍ فَشَرِبَ وَسَقَى أَبَا بَكْرٍ ثُمَّ قَالَ لِلضَّرْعِ اقْلِصْ فَقَلَصَ قَالَ ثُمَّ أَتَيْتُهُ بَعْدَ هَذَا فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ عَلِّمْنِي مِنْ هَذَا الْقَوْلِ قَالَ فَمَسَحَ رَأْسِي وَقَالَ يَرْحَمُكَ اللَّهُ فَإِنَّكَ غُلَيِّمٌ مُعَلَّمٌ حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ عَنْ عَاصِمٍ بِإِسْنَادِهِ قَالَ فَأَتَاهُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ بِصَخْرَةٍ مَنْقُورَةٍ فَاحْتَلَبَ فِيهَا فَشَرِبَ وَشَرِبَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ وَشَرِبْتُ قَالَ ثُمَّ أَتَيْتُهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ قُلْتُ عَلِّمْنِي مِنْ هَذَا الْقُرْآنِ قَالَ إِنَّكَ غُلَامٌ مُعَلَّمٌ قَالَ فَأَخَذْتُ مِنْ فِيهِ سَبْعِينَ سُورَةً
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash, from 'Asim, from Zirr — that Ibn Mas'ud said: 'I was tending the sheep of 'Uqba ibn Abi Mu'ayt when the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) and Abu Bakr passed by me. He said: O boy, do you have any milk? I said: Yes, but I am entrusted [with these animals — the milk is not mine to give]. He said: Is there a sheep that has not been covered by the ram? I brought him a sheep and he wiped its udder — milk flowed. He milked it into a vessel, drank, and gave Abu Bakr to drink. Then he said to the udder: Shrink! and it shrank. Then I came to him after this and said: O Messenger of Allah, teach me from this speech. He wiped my head and said: May Allah have mercy on you — you are a taught young boy [ghulayyim mu'allam].' — [A second chain via 'Affan and Hammad ibn Salama from 'Asim] adds: 'Abu Bakr brought him a hollowed stone, so he milked into it and drank, and Abu Bakr drank, and I drank. Then I came to him afterward and said: Teach me from this Quran. He said: You are a taught youth. And I took from his lips seventy surahs.'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash, from 'Asim, from Zirr — that Ibn Mas'ud said: 'I was tending the sheep of 'Uqba ibn Abi Mu'ayt when Rasulullah ﷺ and Abu Bakr passed by me. He said: O boy, do you have any milk? I said: Yes, but I am entrusted [with these animals — the milk is not mine to give]. He said: Is there a sheep that has not been covered by the ram? I brought him a sheep and he wiped its udder — milk flowed. He milked it into a vessel, drank, and gave Abu Bakr to drink. Then he said to the udder: Shrink! and it shrank. Then I came to him after this and said: O Messenger of Allah, teach me from this speech. He wiped my head and said: May Allah have mercy on you — you are a taught young boy [ghulayyim mu'allam].' — [A second chain via 'Affan and Hammad ibn Salama from 'Asim] adds: 'Abu Bakr brought him a hollowed stone, so he milked into it and drank, and Abu Bakr drank, and I drank. Then I came to him afterward and said: Teach me from this Al-Quran. He said: You are a taught youth. And I took from his lips seventy surahs.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ حَدَّثَنِي عَاصِمٌ عَنْ زِرٍّ عَنْ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ كُنْتُ أَرْعَى غَنَمًا لِعُقْبَةَ بْنِ أَبِي مُعَيْطٍ فَمَرَّ بِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَأَبُو بَكْرٍ فَقَالَ يَا غُلَامُ هَلْ مِنْ لَبَنٍ قَالَ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ وَلَكِنِّي مُؤْتَمَنٌ قَالَ فَهَلْ مِنْ شَاةٍ لَمْ يَنْزُ عَلَيْهَا الْفَحْلُ فَأَتَيْتُهُ بِشَاةٍ فَمَسَحَ ضَرْعَهَا فَنَزَلَ لَبَنٌ فَحَلَبَهُ فِي إِنَاءٍ فَشَرِبَ وَسَقَى أَبَا بَكْرٍ ثُمَّ قَالَ لِلضَّرْعِ اقْلِصْ فَقَلَصَ قَالَ ثُمَّ أَتَيْتُهُ بَعْدَ هَذَا فَقُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ عَلِّ
matn_ar: مْنِي مِنْ هَذَا الْقَوْلِ قَالَ فَمَسَحَ رَأْسِي وَقَالَ يَرْحَمُكَ اللَّهُ فَإِنَّكَ غُلَيِّمٌ مُعَلَّمٌ حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ عَنْ عَاصِمٍ بِإِسْنَادِهِ قَالَ فَأَتَاهُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ بِصَخْرَةٍ مَنْقُورَةٍ فَاحْتَلَبَ فِيهَا فَشَرِبَ وَشَرِبَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ وَشَرِبْتُ قَالَ ثُمَّ أَتَيْتُهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ قُلْتُ عَلِّمْنِي مِنْ هَذَا الْقُرْآنِ قَالَ إِنَّكَ غُلَامٌ مُعَلَّمٌ قَالَ فَأَخَذْتُ مِنْ فِيهِ سَبْعِينَ سُورَةً
matn_ar_clean: مني من هذا القول قال فمسح رأسي وقال يرحمك الله فإنك غليم معلم حدثنا عفان حدثنا حماد بن سلمة عن عاصم بإسناده قال فأتاه أبو بكر بصخرة منقورة فاحتلب فيها فشرب وشرب أبو بكر وشربت قال ثم أتيته بعد ذلك قلت علمني من هذا القرآن قال إنك غلام معلم قال فأخذت من فيه سبعين سورة
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash, from 'Asim, from Zirr — that Ibn Mas'ud said: 'I was tending the sheep of 'Uqba ibn Abi Mu'ayt when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and Abu Bakr passed by me. He said: O boy, do you have any milk? I said: Yes, but I am entrusted [with these animals — the milk is not mine to give]. He said: Is there a sheep that has not been covered by the ram? I brought him a sheep and he wiped its udder — milk flowed. He milked it into a vessel, drank, and gave Abu Bakr to drink. Then he said to the udder: Shrink! and it shrank. Then I came to him after this and said: O Messenger of Allah, teach me from this speech. He wiped my head and said: May Allah have mercy on you — you are a taught young boy [ghulayyim mu'allam].' — [A second chain via 'Affan and Hammad ibn Salama from 'Asim] adds: 'Abu Bakr brought him a hollowed stone, so he milked into it and drank, and Abu Bakr drank, and I drank. Then I came to him afterward and said: Teach me from this Quran. He said: You are a taught youth. And I took from his lips seventy surahs.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَاصِمٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زِرِّ بْنِ حُبَيْشٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّ اللَّهَ نَظَرَ فِي قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ فَوَجَدَ قَلْبَ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏خَيْرَ قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ فَاصْطَفَاهُ لِنَفْسِهِ فَابْتَعَثَهُ بِرِسَالَتِهِ ثُمَّ نَظَرَ فِي قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ بَعْدَ قَلْبِ ‏ ‏مُحَمَّدٍ ‏ ‏فَوَجَدَ قُلُوبَ أَصْحَابِهِ خَيْرَ قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ فَجَعَلَهُمْ وُزَرَاءَ نَبِيِّهِ يُقَاتِلُونَ عَلَى دِينِهِ فَمَا رَأَى الْمُسْلِمُونَ حَسَنًا فَهُوَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ حَسَنٌ وَمَا رَأَوْا سَيِّئًا فَهُوَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ سَيِّئٌ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ حَدَّثَنَا عَاصِمٌ عَنْ زِرِّ بْنِ حُبَيْشٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ نَظَرَ فِي قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ فَوَجَدَ قَلْبَ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ خَيْرَ قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ فَاصْطَفَاهُ لِنَفْسِهِ فَابْتَعَثَهُ بِرِسَالَتِهِ ثُمَّ نَظَرَ فِي قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ بَعْدَ قَلْبِ مُحَمَّدٍ فَوَجَدَ قُلُوبَ أَصْحَابِهِ خَيْرَ قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ فَجَعَلَهُمْ وُزَرَاءَ نَبِيِّهِ يُقَاتِلُونَ عَلَى دِينِهِ فَمَا رَأَى الْمُسْلِمُونَ حَسَنًا فَهُوَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ حَسَنٌ وَمَا رَأَوْا سَيِّئًا فَهُوَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ سَيِّئٌ
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Bakr, from 'Asim, from Zirr ibn Hubaysh — that 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: 'Indeed Allah looked into the hearts of His servants and found the heart of Muhammad (ﷺ) to be the best of the servants' hearts, so He chose him for Himself and sent him with His message. Then He looked again into the hearts of the servants after Muhammad's heart and found the hearts of his Companions to be the best of the servants' hearts, so He made them ministers [wuzara'] of His Prophet — they fight for His religion. So whatever the Muslims consider good is good before Allah, and whatever they consider bad is bad before Allah.'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Bakr, from 'Asim, from Zirr ibn Hubaysh — that 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: 'Indeed Allah looked into the hearts of His servants and found the heart of Muhammad (ﷺ) to be the best of the servants' hearts, so He chose him for Himself and sent him with His message. Then He looked again into the hearts of the servants after Muhammad's heart and found the hearts of his Companions to be the best of the servants' hearts, so He made them ministers [wuzara'] of His Prophet — they fight for His religion. So whatever the Muslims consider good is good before Allah, and whatever they consider bad is bad before Allah.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ حَدَّثَنَا عَاصِمٌ عَنْ زِرِّ بْنِ ح
matn_ar: ُبَيْشٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ نَظَرَ فِي قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ فَوَجَدَ قَلْبَ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ خَيْرَ قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ فَاصْطَفَاهُ لِنَفْسِهِ فَابْتَعَثَهُ بِرِسَالَتِهِ ثُمَّ نَظَرَ فِي قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ بَعْدَ قَلْبِ مُحَمَّدٍ فَوَجَدَ قُلُوبَ أَصْحَابِهِ خَيْرَ قُلُوبِ الْعِبَادِ فَجَعَلَهُمْ وُزَرَاءَ نَبِيِّهِ يُقَاتِلُونَ عَلَى دِينِهِ فَمَا رَأَى الْمُسْلِمُونَ حَسَنًا فَهُوَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ حَسَنٌ وَمَا رَأَوْا سَيِّئًا فَهُوَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ سَيِّئٌ
matn_ar_clean: بيش عن عبد الله بن مسعود قال إن الله نظر في قلوب العباد فوجد قلب محمد صلى الله عليه وسلم خير قلوب العباد فاصطفاه لنفسه فابتعثه برسالته ثم نظر في قلوب العباد بعد قلب محمد فوجد قلوب أصحابه خير قلوب العباد فجعلهم وزراء نبيه يقاتلون على دينه فما رأى المسلمون حسنا فهو عند الله حسن وما رأوا سيئا فهو عند الله سيئ
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Bakr, from 'Asim, from Zirr ibn Hubaysh — that 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: 'Indeed Allah looked into the hearts of His servants and found the heart of Muhammad (ﷺ) to be the best of the servants' hearts, so He chose him for Himself and sent him with His message. Then He looked again into the hearts of the servants after Muhammad's heart and found the hearts of his Companions to be the best of the servants' hearts, so He made them ministers [wuzara'] of His Prophet — they fight for His religion. So whatever the Muslims consider good is good before Allah, and whatever they consider bad is bad before Allah.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو بَكْرٍ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏عَاصِمٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏زِرٍّ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَعَلَّكُمْ سَتُدْرِكُونَ أَقْوَامًا يُصَلُّونَ صَلَاةً لِغَيْرِ وَقْتِهَا فَإِذَا أَدْرَكْتُمُوهُمْ فَصَلُّوا فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ فِي الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي تَعْرِفُونَ ثُمَّ صَلُّوا مَعَهُمْ وَاجْعَلُوهَا ‏ ‏سُبْحَةً ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ حَدَّثَنَا عَاصِمٌ عَنْ زِرٍّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَعَلَّكُمْ سَتُدْرِكُونَ أَقْوَامًا يُصَلُّونَ صَلَاةً لِغَيْرِ وَقْتِهَا فَإِذَا أَدْرَكْتُمُوهُمْ فَصَلُّوا فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ فِي الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي تَعْرِفُونَ ثُمَّ صَلُّوا مَعَهُمْ وَاجْعَلُوهَا سُبْحَةً
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Bakr, from 'Asim, from Zirr — that 'Abdullah said: 'The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: You will likely encounter peoples who pray the prayer outside its [prescribed] time. When you find them, pray in your homes at the time you know [to be correct], then pray with them and make it [the second prayer] a supererogatory act [subha].'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Bakr, from 'Asim, from Zirr — that 'Abdullah said: 'Rasulullah ﷺ said: You will likely encounter peoples who pray the prayer outside its [prescribed] time. When you find them, pray in your homes at the time you know [to be correct], then pray with them and make it [the second prayer] a supererogatory act [subha].'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ حَدَّثَنَا عَاصِمٌ عَنْ
matn_ar: زِرٍّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَعَلَّكُمْ سَتُدْرِكُونَ أَقْوَامًا يُصَلُّونَ صَلَاةً لِغَيْرِ وَقْتِهَا فَإِذَا أَدْرَكْتُمُوهُمْ فَصَلُّوا فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ فِي الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي تَعْرِفُونَ ثُمَّ صَلُّوا مَعَهُمْ وَاجْعَلُوهَا سُبْحَةً
matn_ar_clean: زر عن عبد الله قال قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم لعلكم ستدركون أقواما يصلون صلاة لغير وقتها فإذا أدركتموهم فصلوا في بيوتكم في الوقت الذي تعرفون ثم صلوا معهم واجعلوها سبحة
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Bakr, from 'Asim, from Zirr — that 'Abdullah said: 'the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: You will likely encounter peoples who pray the prayer outside its [prescribed] time. When you find them, pray in your homes at the time you know [to be correct], then pray with them and make it [the second prayer] a supererogatory act [subha].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏جَرِيرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَلْقَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏صَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏صَلَاةً فَلَا أَدْرِي زَادَ أَمْ نَقَصَ فَلَمَّا سَلَّمَ قِيلَ لَهُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ هَلْ حَدَثَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ شَيْءٌ قَالَ لَا وَمَا ذَاكَ قَالُوا صَلَّيْتَ كَذَا وَكَذَا قَالَ فَثَنَى رِجْلَيْهِ فَسَجَدَ سَجْدَتَيْ السَّهْوِ فَلَمَّا سَلَّمَ قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ أَنْسَى كَمَا تَنْسَوْنَ وَإِذَا شَكَّ أَحَدُكُمْ فِي الصَّلَاةِ ‏ ‏فَلْيَتَحَرَّ ‏ ‏الصَّلَاةَ فَإِذَا سَلَّمَ فَلْيَسْجُدْ سَجْدَتَيْنِ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ عَلْقَمَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ صَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ صَلَاةً فَلَا أَدْرِي زَادَ أَمْ نَقَصَ فَلَمَّا سَلَّمَ قِيلَ لَهُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ هَلْ حَدَثَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ شَيْءٌ قَالَ لَا وَمَا ذَاكَ قَالُوا صَلَّيْتَ كَذَا وَكَذَا قَالَ فَثَنَى رِجْلَيْهِ فَسَجَدَ سَجْدَتَيْ السَّهْوِ فَلَمَّا سَلَّمَ قَالَ إِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ أَنْسَى كَمَا تَنْسَوْنَ وَإِذَا شَكَّ أَحَدُكُمْ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَلْيَتَحَرَّ الصَّلَاةَ فَإِذَا سَلَّمَ فَلْيَسْجُدْ سَجْدَتَيْنِ
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, from 'Alqama — that 'Abdullah said: 'The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) prayed a prayer — I do not know whether he added or subtracted [a rak'a]. When he gave the salam, it was said to him: O Messenger of Allah, has something happened in the prayer? He said: No — what is it? They said: You prayed so-and-so many [rak'ahs]. So he folded his legs and prostrated two prostrations of forgetfulness [sujud al-sahw]. When he gave the salam, he said: I am but a human — I forget as you forget. When one of you doubts in his prayer, let him seek what is correct [fataharra], and when he gives the salam, let him prostrate two prostrations.'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, from 'Alqama — that 'Abdullah said: 'Rasulullah ﷺ prayed a prayer — I do not know whether he added or subtracted [a rak'a]. When he gave the salam, it was said to him: O Messenger of Allah, has something happened in the prayer? He said: No — what is it? They said: You prayed so-and-so many [rak'ahs]. So he folded his legs and prostrated two prostrations of forgetfulness [sujud al-sahw]. When he gave the salam, he said: I am but a human — I forget as you forget. When one of you doubts in his prayer, let him seek what is correct [fataharra], and when he gives the salam, let him prostrate two prostrations.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ عَلْقَمَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ صَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ صَلَاةً فَلَا أَدْرِي زَادَ أَمْ نَقَصَ فَلَمَّا سَلَّمَ قِيلَ لَهُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ه
matn_ar: َلْ حَدَثَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ شَيْءٌ قَالَ لَا وَمَا ذَاكَ قَالُوا صَلَّيْتَ كَذَا وَكَذَا قَالَ فَثَنَى رِجْلَيْهِ فَسَجَدَ سَجْدَتَيْ السَّهْوِ فَلَمَّا سَلَّمَ قَالَ إِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ أَنْسَى كَمَا تَنْسَوْنَ وَإِذَا شَكَّ أَحَدُكُمْ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَلْيَتَحَرَّ الصَّلَاةَ فَإِذَا سَلَّمَ فَلْيَسْجُدْ سَجْدَتَيْنِ
matn_ar_clean: ل حدث في الصلاة شيء قال لا وما ذاك قالوا صليت كذا وكذا قال فثنى رجليه فسجد سجدتي السهو فلما سلم قال إنما أنا بشر أنسى كما تنسون وإذا شك أحدكم في الصلاة فليتحر الصلاة فإذا سلم فليسجد سجدتين
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, from 'Alqama — that 'Abdullah said: 'the Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed a prayer — I do not know whether he added or subtracted [a rak'a]. When he gave the salam, it was said to him: O Messenger of Allah, has something happened in the prayer? He said: No — what is it? They said: You prayed so-and-so many [rak'ahs]. So he folded his legs and prostrated two prostrations of forgetfulness [sujud al-sahw]. When he gave the salam, he said: I am but a human — I forget as you forget. When one of you doubts in his prayer, let him seek what is correct [fataharra], and when he gives the salam, let him prostrate two prostrations.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏جَرِيرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏خَيْثَمَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏رَجُلٍ ‏ ‏مِنْ قَوْمِهِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا ‏ ‏سَمَرَ ‏ ‏بَعْدَ الصَّلَاةِ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي الْعِشَاءَ الْآخِرَةَ ‏ ‏إِلَّا لِأَحَدِ رَجُلَيْنِ مُصَلٍّ أَوْ مُسَافِرٍ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ خَيْثَمَةَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ قَوْمِهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَا سَمَرَ بَعْدَ الصَّلَاةِ يَعْنِي الْعِشَاءَ الْآخِرَةَ إِلَّا لِأَحَدِ رَجُلَيْنِ مُصَلٍّ أَوْ مُسَافِرٍ
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from Mansur, from Khaythama, from a man of his people — that 'Abdullah said: 'The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) said: No late-night conversation [samar] after the prayer' — meaning the last 'Isha' — 'except for one of two men: one who is praying, or a traveler.'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from Mansur, from Khaythama, from a man of his people — that 'Abdullah said: 'Rasulullah ﷺ said: No late-night conversation [samar] after the prayer' — meaning the last 'Isha' — 'except for one of two men: one who is praying, or a traveler.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ خَيْثَمَةَ عَنْ رَجُل
matn_ar: ٍ مِنْ قَوْمِهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَا سَمَرَ بَعْدَ الصَّلَاةِ يَعْنِي الْعِشَاءَ الْآخِرَةَ إِلَّا لِأَحَدِ رَجُلَيْنِ مُصَلٍّ أَوْ مُسَافِرٍ
matn_ar_clean: من قومه عن عبد الله قال قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم لا سمر بعد الصلاة يعني العشاء الآخرة إلا لأحد رجلين مصل أو مسافر
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from Mansur, from Khaythama, from a man of his people — that 'Abdullah said: 'the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: No late-night conversation [samar] after the prayer' — meaning the last 'Isha' — 'except for one of two men: one who is praying, or a traveler.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏جَرِيرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَنْصُورٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي وَائِلٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ نَاسٌ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَنُؤَاخَذُ بِأَعْمَالِنَا فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏مَنْ أَحْسَنَ مِنْكُمْ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ فَلَا يُؤَاخَذُ بِهِ وَمَنْ أَسَاءَ فَيُؤْخَذُ بِعَمَلِهِ الْأَوَّلِ وَالْآخِرِ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ نَاسٌ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ أَنُؤَاخَذُ بِأَعْمَالِنَا فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فَقَالَ مَنْ أَحْسَنَ مِنْكُمْ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ فَلَا يُؤَاخَذُ بِهِ وَمَنْ أَسَاءَ فَيُؤْخَذُ بِعَمَلِهِ الْأَوَّلِ وَالْآخِرِ
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from Mansur, from Abi Wa'il — that 'Abdullah said: 'Some people said: O Messenger of Allah, will we be held accountable for our deeds in the Jahiliyya? He said: Whoever among you does good in Islam will not be held accountable for it [the Jahiliyya]. And whoever does evil will be held accountable for both his first and last [deeds].'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from Mansur, from Abi Wa'il — that 'Abdullah said: 'Some people said: O Messenger of Allah, will we be held accountable for our deeds in the Jahiliyya? He said: Whoever among you does good in Islam will not be held accountable for it [the Jahiliyya]. And whoever does evil will be held accountable for both his first and last [deeds].'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ نَاسٌ يَا رَس
matn_ar: ُولَ اللَّهِ أَنُؤَاخَذُ بِأَعْمَالِنَا فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ فَقَالَ مَنْ أَحْسَنَ مِنْكُمْ فِي الْإِسْلَامِ فَلَا يُؤَاخَذُ بِهِ وَمَنْ أَسَاءَ فَيُؤْخَذُ بِعَمَلِهِ الْأَوَّلِ وَالْآخِرِ
matn_ar_clean: ول الله أنؤاخذ بأعمالنا في الجاهلية فقال من أحسن منكم في الإسلام فلا يؤاخذ به ومن أساء فيؤخذ بعمله الأول والآخر
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from Mansur, from Abi Wa'il — that 'Abdullah said: 'Some people said: O Messenger of Allah, will we be held accountable for our deeds in the Jahiliyya? He said: Whoever among you does good in Islam will not be held accountable for it [the Jahiliyya]. And whoever does evil will be held accountable for both his first and last [deeds].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏جَرِيرٌ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏الرُّكَيْنِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمِّهِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ حَرْمَلَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَكْرَهُ عَشْرَ ‏ ‏خِلَالٍ ‏ ‏تَخَتُّمَ الذَّهَبِ وَجَرَّ ‏ ‏الْإِزَارِ ‏ ‏وَالصُّفْرَةَ ‏ ‏يَعْنِي ‏ ‏الْخَلُوقَ ‏ ‏وَتَغْيِيرَ الشَّيْبِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَرِيرٌ ‏ ‏إِنَّمَا ‏ ‏يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ نَتْفَهُ ‏ ‏وَعَزْلَ ‏ ‏الْمَاءِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏مَحِلِّهِ ‏ ‏وَالرُّقَى إِلَّا ‏ ‏بِالْمُعَوِّذَاتِ ‏ ‏وَفَسَادَ الصَّبِيِّ غَيْرَ مُحَرِّمِهِ وَعَقْدَ ‏ ‏التَّمَائِمِ ‏ ‏وَالتَّبَرُّجَ بِالزِّينَةِ لِغَيْرِ مَحِلِّهَا وَالضَّرْبَ بِالْكِعَابِ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ عَنْ الرُّكَيْنِ عَنْ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ عَمِّهِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ حَرْمَلَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَكْرَهُ عَشْرَ خِلَالٍ تَخَتُّمَ الذَّهَبِ وَجَرَّ الْإِزَارِ وَالصُّفْرَةَ يَعْنِي الْخَلُوقَ وَتَغْيِيرَ الشَّيْبِ قَالَ جَرِيرٌ إِنَّمَا يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ نَتْفَهُ وَعَزْلَ الْمَاءِ عَنْ مَحِلِّهِ وَالرُّقَى إِلَّا بِالْمُعَوِّذَاتِ وَفَسَادَ الصَّبِيِّ غَيْرَ مُحَرِّمِهِ وَعَقْدَ التَّمَائِمِ وَالتَّبَرُّجَ بِالزِّينَةِ لِغَيْرِ مَحِلِّهَا وَالضَّرْبَ بِالْكِعَابِ
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from al-Rukayn, from al-Qasim ibn Hassan, from his paternal uncle 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Harmala — that 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: 'The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) disliked ten things: wearing a gold ring [takhattum al-dhahab], dragging the lower garment [jarr al-izar], [using] yellow saffron perfume [al-sufra, meaning al-khaluq], removing grey hair [Jarir explained: meaning plucking it out], coitus interruptus ['azl] in an unlawful manner, incantations [ruqa] except with the protective surahs [al-mu'awwidhat], harming an infant [by premature weaning] without [the husband] making it unlawful [i.e., without consent], tying amulets [tamimah], displaying adornment before those not entitled to see it, and gambling with dice [al-darb bil-ki'ab].'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from al-Rukayn, from al-Qasim ibn Hassan, from his paternal uncle 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Harmala — that 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: 'Rasulullah ﷺ disliked ten things: wearing a gold ring [takhattum al-dhahab], dragging the lower garment [jarr al-izar], [using] yellow saffron perfume [al-sufra, meaning al-khaluq], removing grey hair [Jarir explained: meaning plucking it out], coitus interruptus ['azl] in an unlawful manner, incantations [ruqa] except with the protective surahs [al-mu'awwidhat], harming an infant [by premature weaning] without [the husband] making it unlawful [i.e., without consent], tying amulets [tamimah], displaying adornment before those not entitled to see it, and gambling with dice [al-darb bil-ki'ab].'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ عَنْ الرُّكَيْنِ عَنْ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ عَمِّهِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ حَرْمَلَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ ع
matn_ar: َلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَكْرَهُ عَشْرَ خِلَالٍ تَخَتُّمَ الذَّهَبِ وَجَرَّ الْإِزَارِ وَالصُّفْرَةَ يَعْنِي الْخَلُوقَ وَتَغْيِيرَ الشَّيْبِ قَالَ جَرِيرٌ إِنَّمَا يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ نَتْفَهُ وَعَزْلَ الْمَاءِ عَنْ مَحِلِّهِ وَالرُّقَى إِلَّا بِالْمُعَوِّذَاتِ وَفَسَادَ الصَّبِيِّ غَيْرَ مُحَرِّمِهِ وَعَقْدَ التَّمَائِمِ وَالتَّبَرُّجَ بِالزِّينَةِ لِغَيْرِ مَحِلِّهَا وَالضَّرْبَ بِالْكِعَابِ
matn_ar_clean: ليه وسلم يكره عشر خلال تختم الذهب وجر الإزار والصفرة يعني الخلوق وتغيير الشيب قال جرير إنما يعني بذلك نتفه وعزل الماء عن محله والرقى إلا بالمعوذات وفساد الصبي غير محرمه وعقد التمائم والتبرج بالزينة لغير محلها والضرب بالكعاب
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Jarir, from al-Rukayn, from al-Qasim ibn Hassan, from his paternal uncle 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Harmala — that 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: 'the Messenger of Allah ﷺ disliked ten things: wearing a gold ring [takhattum al-dhahab], dragging the lower garment [jarr al-izar], [using] yellow saffron perfume [al-sufra, meaning al-khaluq], removing grey hair [Jarir explained: meaning plucking it out], coitus interruptus ['azl] in an unlawful manner, incantations [ruqa] except with the protective surahs [al-mu'awwidhat], harming an infant [by premature weaning] without [the husband] making it unlawful [i.e., without consent], tying amulets [tamimah], displaying adornment before those not entitled to see it, and gambling with dice [al-darb bil-ki'ab].'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏يَحْيَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏سُفْيَانَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏إِبْرَاهِيمَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عُبَيْدَةَ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏سُلَيْمَانُ ‏ ‏وَبَعْضُ الْحَدِيثِ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي ‏ ‏أَبِي ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏أَبِي الضُّحَى ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ النَّبِيُّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏اقْرَأْ عَلَيَّ قَالَ قُلْتُ أَقْرَأُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَيْكَ أُنْزِلَ قَالَ ‏ ‏إِنَّنِي أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَسْمَعَهُ مِنْ غَيْرِي فَقَرَأْتُ حَتَّى إِذَا بَلَغْتُ ‏ { ‏فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ عَلَى هَؤُلَاءِ شَهِيدًا ‏} ‏قَالَ رَأَيْتُ عَيْنَيْهِ ‏ ‏تَذْرِفَانِ ‏ ‏دُمُوعًا ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى عَنْ سُفْيَانَ حَدَّثَنِي سُلَيْمَانُ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ عُبَيْدَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ سُلَيْمَانُ وَبَعْضُ الْحَدِيثِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي عَنْ أَبِي الضُّحَى عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ اقْرَأْ عَلَيَّ قَالَ قُلْتُ أَقْرَأُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَيْكَ أُنْزِلَ قَالَ إِنَّنِي أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَسْمَعَهُ مِنْ غَيْرِي فَقَرَأْتُ حَتَّى إِذَا بَلَغْتُ { فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ عَلَى هَؤُلَاءِ شَهِيدًا } قَالَ رَأَيْتُ عَيْنَيْهِ تَذْرِفَانِ دُمُوعًا
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Yahya, from Sufyan, from Sulayman, from Ibrahim, from 'Ubayda (and some of the narration is via 'Amr ibn Murra, from his father, from Abi al-Duha) — that 'Abdullah said: 'The Prophet (ﷺ) said: Recite to me. I said: Shall I recite to you when it was revealed to you? He said: I love to hear it from someone else. So I recited until when I reached: How then will it be when We bring from every nation a witness, and We bring you [O Muhammad] against these as a witness? [4:41] — he [Ibn Mas'ud] said: I saw his two eyes flowing with tears.'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Yahya, from Sufyan, from Sulayman, from Ibrahim, from 'Ubayda (and some of the narration is via 'Amr ibn Murra, from his father, from Abi al-Duha) — that 'Abdullah said: 'Nabi ﷺ said: Recite to me. I said: Shall I recite to you when it was revealed to you? He said: I love to hear it from someone else. So I recited until when I reached: How then will it be when We bring from every nation a witness, and We bring you [O Muhammad] against these as a witness? [4:41] — he [Ibn Mas'ud] said: I saw his two eyes flowing with tears.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى عَنْ سُفْيَانَ حَدَّثَنِي سُلَيْمَانُ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ عُبَيْدَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ سُلَيْمَانُ وَبَعْضُ الْحَدِيثِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي عَنْ أَبِي الضُّحَى عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ اقْرَأْ عَلَيَّ قَالَ قُلْ
matn_ar: تُ أَقْرَأُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَيْكَ أُنْزِلَ قَالَ إِنَّنِي أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَسْمَعَهُ مِنْ غَيْرِي فَقَرَأْتُ حَتَّى إِذَا بَلَغْتُ { فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ عَلَى هَؤُلَاءِ شَهِيدًا } قَالَ رَأَيْتُ عَيْنَيْهِ تَذْرِفَانِ دُمُوعًا
matn_ar_clean: ت أقرأ عليك وعليك أنزل قال إنني أحب أن أسمعه من غيري فقرأت حتى إذا بلغت { فكيف إذا جئنا من كل أمة بشهيد وجئنا بك على هؤلاء شهيدا } قال رأيت عينيه تذرفان دموعا
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Yahya, from Sufyan, from Sulayman, from Ibrahim, from 'Ubayda (and some of the narration is via 'Amr ibn Murra, from his father, from Abi al-Duha) — that 'Abdullah said: 'the Prophet ﷺ said: Recite to me. I said: Shall I recite to you when it was revealed to you? He said: I love to hear it from someone else. So I recited until when I reached: How then will it be when We bring from every nation a witness, and We bring you [O Muhammad] against these as a witness? [4:41] — he [Ibn Mas'ud] said: I saw his two eyes flowing with tears.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شَقِيقِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏مِنْ ‏ ‏بَنِي بَجِيلَةَ ‏ ‏يُقَالُ لَهُ ‏ ‏نَهِيكُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ ‏ ‏فَقَالَ يَا ‏ ‏أَبَا عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ ‏ ‏كَيْفَ تَقْرَأُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ أَيَاءً تَجِدُهَا أَوْ أَلِفًا ‏ { ‏مِنْ مَاءٍ غَيْرِ ‏ ‏آسِنٍ ‏ } ‏أَوْ ‏ { ‏غَيْرِ ‏ ‏يَاسِنٍ ‏ } ‏فَقَالَ لَهُ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏أَوَكُلَّ الْقُرْآنِ أَحْصَيْتَ غَيْرَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ قَالَ إِنَّنِي لَأَقْرَأُ ‏ ‏الْمُفَصَّلَ ‏ ‏فِي رَكْعَةٍ فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عَبْدُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏هَذًّا كَهَذِّ الشِّعْرِ إِنَّ مِنْ أَحْسَنِ الصَّلَاةِ الرُّكُوعَ وَالسُّجُودَ وَلَيَقْرَأَنَّ الْقُرْآنَ أَقْوَامٌ لَا يُجَاوِزُ ‏ ‏تَرَاقِيَهُمْ وَلَكِنَّهُ إِذَا قَرَأَهُ ‏ ‏فَرَسَخَ ‏ ‏فِي الْقَلْبِ نَفَعَ إِنِّي لَأَعْرِفُ النَّظَائِرَ الَّتِي كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقْرَأُ سُورَتَيْنِ فِي رَكْعَةٍ قَالَ ثُمَّ قَامَ فَدَخَلَ فَجَاءَ ‏ ‏عَلْقَمَةُ ‏ ‏فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ فَقُلْنَا لَهُ سَلْهُ لَنَا عَنْ النَّظَائِرِ الَّتِي كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏يَقْرَأُ سُورَتَيْنِ فِي رَكْعَةٍ قَالَ فَدَخَلَ فَسَأَلَهُ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ إِلَيْنَا فَقَالَ ‏ ‏عِشْرُونَ سُورَةً مِنْ أَوَّلِ ‏ ‏الْمُفَصَّلِ ‏ ‏فِي تَأْلِيفِ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ عَنْ شَقِيقِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ قَالَ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ مِنْ بَنِي بَجِيلَةَ يُقَالُ لَهُ نَهِيكُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ فَقَالَ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ كَيْفَ تَقْرَأُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ أَيَاءً تَجِدُهَا أَوْ أَلِفًا { مِنْ مَاءٍ غَيْرِ آسِنٍ } أَوْ { غَيْرِ يَاسِنٍ } فَقَالَ لَهُ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ أَوَكُلَّ الْقُرْآنِ أَحْصَيْتَ غَيْرَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ قَالَ إِنَّنِي لَأَقْرَأُ الْمُفَصَّلَ فِي رَكْعَةٍ فَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ هَذًّا كَهَذِّ الشِّعْرِ إِنَّ مِنْ أَحْسَنِ الصَّلَاةِ الرُّكُوعَ وَالسُّجُودَ وَلَيَقْرَأَنَّ الْقُرْآنَ أَقْوَامٌ لَا يُجَاوِزُ تَرَاقِيَهُمْ وَلَكِنَّهُ إِذَا قَرَأَهُ فَرَسَخَ فِي الْقَلْبِ نَفَعَ إِنِّي لَأَعْرِفُ النَّظَائِرَ الَّتِي كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقْرَأُ سُورَتَيْنِ فِي رَكْعَةٍ قَالَ ثُمَّ قَامَ فَدَخَلَ فَجَاءَ عَلْقَمَةُ فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ فَقُلْنَا لَهُ سَلْهُ لَنَا عَنْ النَّظَائِرِ الَّتِي كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقْرَأُ سُورَتَيْنِ فِي رَكْعَةٍ قَالَ فَدَخَلَ فَسَأَلَهُ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ إِلَيْنَا فَقَالَ عِشْرُونَ سُورَةً مِنْ أَوَّلِ الْمُفَصَّلِ فِي تَأْلِيفِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Mu'awiya, from al-A'mash, from Shaqiq ibn Salama — that he said: 'A man came to 'Abdullah [ibn Mas'ud] from the tribe of Bajila, called Nahik ibn Sinan. He said: O Abu 'Abd al-Rahman, how do you read this verse — do you find it with a ya or an alif: from water that does not alter [asin/yasin] [47:15]? 'Abdullah said to him: Have you memorized all the Quran except for this one verse? [The man] said: I recite all of al-Mufassal [the shorter surahs] in one rak'a. 'Abdullah said: [That is] rapid like the reciting of poetry! Indeed among the best of prayer are the bowing [ruku'] and the prostration [sujud]. And there will come peoples who recite the Quran but it does not pass beyond their collar-bones. But when one recites it and it becomes firmly rooted in the heart, it benefits [him]. I know the paired surahs [al-naza'ir] that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to recite — two surahs in one rak'a. He then stood up and went inside. 'Alqama came and entered upon him. [The companions] said: Ask him for us about the pairs that the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) used to recite — two surahs in one rak'a. He entered and asked him, then came back out to us and said: Twenty surahs from the beginning of al-Mufassal, in the arrangement of 'Abdullah.'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Mu'awiya, from al-A'mash, from Shaqiq ibn Salama — that he said: 'A man came to 'Abdullah [ibn Mas'ud] from the tribe of Bajila, called Nahik ibn Sinan. He said: O Abu 'Abd al-Rahman, how do you read this verse — do you find it with a ya or an alif: from water that does not alter [asin/yasin] [47:15]? 'Abdullah said to him: Have you memorized all the Al-Quran except for this one verse? [The man] said: I recite all of al-Mufassal [the shorter surahs] in one rak'a. 'Abdullah said: [That is] rapid like the reciting of poetry! Indeed among the best of prayer are the bowing [ruku'] and the prostration [sujud]. And there will come peoples who recite the Al-Quran but it does not pass beyond their collar-bones. But when one recites it and it becomes firmly rooted in the heart, it benefits [him]. I know the paired surahs [al-naza'ir] that Rasulullah ﷺ used to recite — two surahs in one rak'a. He then stood up and went inside. 'Alqama came and entered upon him. [The companions] said: Ask him for us about the pairs that Rasulullah ﷺ used to recite — two surahs in one rak'a. He entered and asked him, then came back out to us and said: Twenty surahs from the beginning of al-Mufassal, in the arrangement of 'Abdullah.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ عَنْ شَقِيقِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ قَالَ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ مِنْ بَنِي بَجِيلَةَ يُقَالُ لَهُ نَهِيكُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ فَقَالَ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ كَيْفَ تَقْرَأُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ أَيَاءً تَجِدُهَا أَوْ أَلِفًا { مِنْ مَاءٍ غَيْرِ آسِنٍ } أَوْ { غَيْرِ يَاسِنٍ } فَقَالَ لَهُ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ أَوَكُلَّ الْقُرْآنِ أَحْصَيْتَ غَيْرَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ قَالَ إِنَّنِي لَأَقْرَأُ الْمُفَصَّلَ فِي رَكْعَةٍ فَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ هَذًّا كَهَذِّ الشِّعْرِ إِنَّ مِنْ أَحْسَنِ الصَّلَاةِ الرُّكُوعَ وَالسُّجُودَ وَلَيَقْرَأَنَّ الْقُرْآنَ أَقْوَامٌ لَا يُجَاوِزُ تَرَاقِيَهُمْ وَلَكِنَّهُ إِذ
matn_ar: َا قَرَأَهُ فَرَسَخَ فِي الْقَلْبِ نَفَعَ إِنِّي لَأَعْرِفُ النَّظَائِرَ الَّتِي كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقْرَأُ سُورَتَيْنِ فِي رَكْعَةٍ قَالَ ثُمَّ قَامَ فَدَخَلَ فَجَاءَ عَلْقَمَةُ فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ فَقُلْنَا لَهُ سَلْهُ لَنَا عَنْ النَّظَائِرِ الَّتِي كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقْرَأُ سُورَتَيْنِ فِي رَكْعَةٍ قَالَ فَدَخَلَ فَسَأَلَهُ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ إِلَيْنَا فَقَالَ عِشْرُونَ سُورَةً مِنْ أَوَّلِ الْمُفَصَّلِ فِي تَأْلِيفِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ
matn_ar_clean: ا قرأه فرسخ في القلب نفع إني لأعرف النظائر التي كان رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقرأ سورتين في ركعة قال ثم قام فدخل فجاء علقمة فدخل عليه قال فقلنا له سله لنا عن النظائر التي كان رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم يقرأ سورتين في ركعة قال فدخل فسأله ثم خرج إلينا فقال عشرون سورة من أول المفصل في تأليف عبد الله
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Mu'awiya, from al-A'mash, from Shaqiq ibn Salama — that he said: 'A man came to 'Abdullah [ibn Mas'ud] from the tribe of Bajila, called Nahik ibn Sinan. He said: O Abu 'Abd al-Rahman, how do you read this verse — do you find it with a ya or an alif: from water that does not alter [asin/yasin] [47:15]? 'Abdullah said to him: Have you memorized all the Quran except for this one verse? [The man] said: I recite all of al-Mufassal [the shorter surahs] in one rak'a. 'Abdullah said: [That is] rapid like the reciting of poetry! Indeed among the best of prayer are the bowing [ruku'] and the prostration [sujud]. And there will come peoples who recite the Quran but it does not pass beyond their collar-bones. But when one recites it and it becomes firmly rooted in the heart, it benefits [him]. I know the paired surahs [al-naza'ir] that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite — two surahs in one rak'a. He then stood up and went inside. 'Alqama came and entered upon him. [The companions] said: Ask him for us about the pairs that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite — two surahs in one rak'a. He entered and asked him, then came back out to us and said: Twenty surahs from the beginning of al-Mufassal, in the arrangement of 'Abdullah.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شَقِيقٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَسَمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ قَسْمًا قَالَ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ ‏ ‏الْأَنْصَارِ ‏ ‏إِنَّ هَذِهِ لَقِسْمَةٌ مَا أُرِيدَ بِهَا وَجْهُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ يَا عَدُوَّ اللَّهِ أَمَا لَأُخْبِرَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏بِمَا قُلْتَ قَالَ فَذَكَرَ ذَلِكَ لِلنَّبِيِّ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏فَاحْمَرَّ وَجْهُهُ قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ ‏ ‏رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ عَلَى ‏ ‏مُوسَى ‏ ‏لَقَدْ أُوذِيَ بِأَكْثَرَ مِنْ هَذَا فَصَبَرَ ‏
ar_clean: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ عَنْ شَقِيقٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَسَمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ قَسْمًا قَالَ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ الْأَنْصَارِ إِنَّ هَذِهِ لَقِسْمَةٌ مَا أُرِيدَ بِهَا وَجْهُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ يَا عَدُوَّ اللَّهِ أَمَا لَأُخْبِرَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِمَا قُلْتَ قَالَ فَذَكَرَ ذَلِكَ لِلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَاحْمَرَّ وَجْهُهُ قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ عَلَى مُوسَى لَقَدْ أُوذِيَ بِأَكْثَرَ مِنْ هَذَا فَصَبَرَ
sunnah_ref_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Mu'awiya, from al-A'mash, from Shaqiq — that 'Abdullah said: 'The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) made a distribution one day. A man from the Ansar said: This is a distribution that was not intended for the face of Allah (عز وجل). I said: O enemy of Allah, I will certainly inform the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) of what you said. He [Ibn Mas'ud] mentioned that to the Prophet (ﷺ) and his face turned red. Then he said: May Allah have mercy on Musa — he was harmed with more than this yet he remained patient.'
iwh_id: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Mu'awiya, from al-A'mash, from Shaqiq — that 'Abdullah said: 'Rasulullah ﷺ made a distribution one day. A man from the Ansar said: This is a distribution that was not intended for the face of Allah (عز وجل). I said: O enemy of Allah, I will certainly inform Rasulullah ﷺ of what you said. He [Ibn Mas'ud] mentioned that to Nabi ﷺ and his face turned red. Then he said: May Allah have mercy on Musa — he was harmed with more than this yet he remained patient.'
isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ عَنْ شَقِيقٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ قَسَمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ قَسْمًا قَالَ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ الْأَنْصَارِ إِنَّ هَذِهِ لَقِسْمَةٌ مَا أُرِيدَ بِهَا وَجْهُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ قَالَ فَقُل
matn_ar: ْتُ يَا عَدُوَّ اللَّهِ أَمَا لَأُخْبِرَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِمَا قُلْتَ قَالَ فَذَكَرَ ذَلِكَ لِلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَاحْمَرَّ وَجْهُهُ قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ عَلَى مُوسَى لَقَدْ أُوذِيَ بِأَكْثَرَ مِنْ هَذَا فَصَبَرَ
matn_ar_clean: ت يا عدو الله أما لأخبرن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم بما قلت قال فذكر ذلك للنبي صلى الله عليه وسلم فاحمر وجهه قال ثم قال رحمة الله على موسى لقد أوذي بأكثر من هذا فصبر
iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (رضي الله عنه) — via Abu Mu'awiya, from al-A'mash, from Shaqiq — that 'Abdullah said: 'the Messenger of Allah ﷺ made a distribution one day. A man from the Ansar said: This is a distribution that was not intended for the face of Allah (عز وجل). I said: O enemy of Allah, I will certainly inform the Messenger of Allah ﷺ of what you said. He [Ibn Mas'ud] mentioned that to the Prophet ﷺ and his face turned red. Then he said: May Allah have mercy on Musa — he was harmed with more than this yet he remained patient.'

ar: ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ ‏ ‏حَدَّثَنَا ‏ ‏الْأَعْمَشُ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏شَقِيقٍ ‏ ‏عَنْ ‏ ‏عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏قَالَ ‏ ‏قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏لَا تُبَاشِرْ الْمَرْأَةُ الْمَرْأَةَ حَتَّى تَصِفَهَا لِزَوْجِهَا كَأَنَّمَا يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهَا ‏
